world.title: Djibouti, Somaliland, Ethiopia: A Strategic Triangle in the Horn of Africa
world.description: The Horn of Africa, located at the entrance of the Red Sea and along one of the world’s busiest maritime routes, has once again become a focal point of geopolitical competition. The evolving relations between Djibouti, Somaliland (secessionist region of Somalia since 1991) and Ethiopia illustrate the complex interplay of economic dependency, regional rivalries, and shifting alliances. At the center of this dynamic lies a strategic question: access to the sea and control of regional trade corridors.

science.title: Europe to negotiate new lunar role with NASA after Gateway shift
science.description: ESA seeks to secure European moonwalks after NASA suspends its Gateway station, requiring urgent talks on repurposing promised astronaut seats.

science.title: Scientists discover “molecular shredder” that helps deadly parasite evade the immune system
science.description: A deadly parasite responsible for sleeping sickness has been found using a surprisingly precise trick to stay hidden in the human bloodstream. Scientists discovered a protein called ESB2 that acts like a “molecular shredder,” cutting up specific genetic instructions as they are produced. This allows the parasite to flood its surface with protective proteins while suppressing other signals that might give it away.

ai.title: From AFP’s Digital Investigations team: What's real anymore? AI warps truth of Middle East war
ai.description: AFP's global network has produced more than 500 debunks of false information in multiple languages since the conflict began -- a rate never before seen in such a crisis. Between a quarter and a fifth of them used AI.
